title: asplenium fontanum subsp. fontanum
intro: Asplenium fontanum subsp. fontanum, also known as the ivy spleenwort, is a species of fern belonging to the Aspleniaceae family, characterized by its delicate, lance-shaped fronds and its ability to grow on rocks and trees. The ivy spleenwort has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential treatment for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe and calm the nervous system, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In traditional herbal preparations, the ivy spleenwort is often used as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for a range of health issues.
